gaps.title: Test mocking strategy causes false passes: evidence not persisted to disk
gaps.description: run_gate_collectors persists evidence to disk via run_single_collector -> persist_evidence_record. When _run_collectors is mocked, no evidence reaches disk, so evaluate_gate -> load_evidence_bundle finds nothing and fails closed (all FAIL). Tests in Tasks 8, 15, 16 originally set mock_run.return_value to evidence dicts, but the return value is unused by run_production_gate — evidence is consumed from disk by evaluate_gate.
gaps.fix: Tests must persist evidence to disk via persist_evidence_record in setUp/arrange, then mock _run_collectors to no-op (preventing real subprocess calls). The mock return value is irrelevant.

gaps.title: Risk-9 immediate park not handled in route_gate_verdict
gaps.description: Spec §9.1 says 'exhausted/risk-9 persists -> PARK+escalate'. The original plan only parked when remediation_cycle >= max_cycles (exhaustion), but did not handle the case where has_unmitigated_risk_9 is true — which should park immediately without trying remediation, since risk-9 is a risk assessment issue not fixable by code remediation.
gaps.fix: Added has_unmitigated_risk_9 parameter to route_gate_verdict. When true and overall is FAIL, parks immediately with reason 'risk-9' before checking max_cycles. Added test_fail_risk_9_parks_immediately test.

gaps.title: _run_collectors wrapper missing diff_categories passthrough
gaps.description: The _run_collectors wrapper in gate_orchestrator.py omitted the diff_categories parameter that run_gate_collectors accepts. While M10 doesn't use diff-scoping by default, the wrapper should forward it for future callers.
gaps.fix: Added diff_categories=None parameter to _run_collectors wrapper and forward it to run_gate_collectors.

gaps.title: Task 2 tests missing setUp and _write_gate_file helper
gaps.description: ProductionReadyGateVerifierTests referenced self.project_root without defining setUp, and used self._write_gate_file without implementing the helper. Tests would fail at runtime.
gaps.fix: Added setUp with tempfile.mkdtemp(), and _write_gate_file helper that uses make_gate_file + persist_gate_file.

gaps.title: Remediator dispatch is signal-only — no direct invocation
gaps.description: The plan's route_gate_verdict returns {action: 'remediate'} as a signal to the caller, but does not directly invoke the BMAD review_continuation -> dev-story remediation loop. This is architecturally correct — the existing orchestrator loop in orchestrator.py handles the actual dispatch — but the plan should be explicit that M10 provides the verdict-routing signal, not the full remediation loop.
gaps.fix: No code change needed. The signal-based design is correct — M10 returns the routing decision, the orchestrator loop acts on it.

gaps.title: run_gate_collectors returns CollectorOutcome, not evidence dicts
gaps.description: run_gate_collectors returns list[CollectorOutcome] (with .config, .evidence, .persisted_path), not list[dict]. The run_production_gate function correctly ignores the return value — evidence is consumed from disk by evaluate_gate via load_evidence_bundle. No interface mismatch in production code, only in the original test mocks (addressed by GAP-01).
gaps.fix: No production code change needed. Test mocking fixed in GAP-01.
